text: Maximize Your Space with Attached Garage Conversions and ADUs An Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) is a self-contained living space built on the same lot as your primary home. Whether attached, detached, or integrated into your existing house, each option offers unique advantages depending on your property, budget, and long-term goals. Add a private living space above your garage while preserving your yard and parking. A smart solution for maximizing limited lot space. A detached ADU is a standalone home with its own entrance, kitchen, bathroom, and living space. It offers maximum privacy and excellent rental income potential. A JADU transforms part of your existing home into a self-contained space with its own entrance and kitchenette. It benefits from California's simplified permitting process. ​ An attached ADU shares a wall with the main house while maintaining a private entrance and full living amenities. It offers a cost-effective way to expand your property. Convert your existing garage into a fully permitted ADU. Since the structure already exists, projects are typically faster and more affordable. ​ ​ ​ A two-story ADU maximizes living space by building vertically, making it ideal for smaller lots while preserving valuable outdoor areas. ​ ​ A fully independent unit built in your backyard. Build up, not out — and keep your parking. Homeowners with a detached garage who want to maximize their property's potential. Best for: Homeowners with available backyard space who want maximum rental income or a fully private family suite. Best for: Convert existing space into a functional living unit. Homeowners who want an affordable ADU using existing interior space. Best for: Attached ADU More space, connected to your home. Homeowners who want to add living space or rental income without using backyard square footage. Best for: Your most cost-effective path to rental income. Homeowners with an unused garage who want the quickest and most cost-effective ADU option. Best for: Detached ADU Above-Garage ADU Double the space without expanding your footprint. Homeowners who need more square footage without sacrificing backyard space. Best for: Garage Conversion JADU Two-Story ADU

An Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) is a self-contained living space built on the same lot as your primary home. Whether attached, detached, or integrated into your existing house, each option offers unique advantages depending on your property, budget, and long-term goals.

Add a private living space above your garage while preserving your yard and parking. A smart solution for maximizing limited lot space. A detached ADU is a standalone home with its own entrance, kitchen, bathroom, and living space. It offers maximum privacy and excellent rental income potential. A JADU transforms part of your existing home into a self-contained space with its own entrance and kitchenette. It benefits from California's simplified permitting process. ​ An attached ADU shares a wall with the main house while maintaining a private entrance and full living amenities. It offers a cost-effective way to expand your property. Convert your existing garage into a fully permitted ADU. Since the structure already exists, projects are typically faster and more affordable. ​ ​ ​ A two-story ADU maximizes living space by building vertically, making it ideal for smaller lots while preserving valuable outdoor areas. ​ ​ A fully independent unit built in your backyard. Build up, not out — and keep your parking. Homeowners with a detached garage who want to maximize their property's potential. Homeowners who want an affordable ADU using existing interior space. Best for: Attached ADU More space, connected to your home. Thinking About Building an ADU?
Dedicated Project Manager
CA Lic. #1002927. Your property is protected from day one. ​

The construction industry has a reputation problem. Between confusing city zoning laws, contractors who disappear mid-project, and quotes that balloon after the concrete is poured, it's no surprise homeowners hesitate. ​ An ADU is a $150,000+ investment. You deserve a contractor who treats your home with respect and your budget with transparency. ​ That's why every project with MY Home Builders starts with a fixed, written scope before we break ground, a dedicated project manager from day one, and a team that's licensed, bonded, and insured. Not a subcontractor roulette. ​
Your written quote is your final price. No hidden fees, no surprise change orders. ​
Build now, pay over time. ​
